Therefore, the LTL formula translates to 'whenever the sensor data is not good, the autopilot shall transition to MANEUVER'. In terms of LTL operators, this translates to G(!good -> (F state_is_MANEUVER)).

explanation: The phrase "sensor data is not good" maps to the negation of the atomic proposition "good", i.e., !good. The autopilot changing states from NOMINAL to MANEUVER implies that the autopilot needs to be in NOMINAL mode and then move to MANEUVER mode. This is represented by the LTL formula "state_is_NOMINAL & X state_is_MANEUVER".  The phrase "when" signifies that the state change happens after the condition "!good" is met. Therefore, we combine the condition and the state change using the implication operator "->". This results in the final LTL formula "G(!good -> (state_is_NOMINAL & X state_is_MANEUVER))" which means "globally, if the sensor data is not good, then the autopilot will be in NOMINAL mode and then in MANEUVER mode in the next state."
